codeReview.summary: Implementation provides Project/User/Task memory blocks with auto-loading via DefaultBlockLoader and context assembly via ContextAssembler. The blocks.rs module is well-structured with 7 passing tests. Code follows existing project patterns.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature specifies skillName 'Rust' but worker transcript shows attempt to use 'rust-worker' skill (which doesn't exist). Worker correctly used the available 'Rust' skill instead. The skill feedback correctly notes this mismatch, suggesting the rust-worker skill should be created or renamed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json shows skillName: 'Rust'; transcript shows: 'The rust-worker skill was not found, so I invoked the Rust skill instead'; skillFeedback.deviations: {step: 'skill invocation', whatIDidInstead: 'The rust-worker skill was not found, so I invoked the Rust skill instead'}

sharedStateObservations.observation: The load_blocks function in DefaultBlockLoader uses get_by_type(MemoryBlockType::Project) without repository scope filtering, while get_by_label is used for User and Task blocks. This inconsistency could return project entries from wrong repositories if multiple repos store project blocks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: blocks.rs:52-65 - project block lookup uses get_by_type and iterates checking metadata.repository, but get_by_type itself is not repository-scoped in the MemoryStore trait implementation (lib.rs:get_by_type has no repository parameter)

sharedStateObservations.observation: create_project_block hardcodes the label to 'project:architecture' for ALL project blocks, meaning only one project block per repository. The feature description mentions 'Project (architecture, conventions)' suggesting possibly multiple project-related blocks. If a repository needs separate architecture vs conventions blocks, the current design doesn't support this.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: blocks.rs:167 - label: labels::PROJECT_ARCHITECTURE.to_string() is hardcoded; labels module defines PROJECT_CONVENTIONS constant but it's not used

summary: Feature memory-memory-blocks passes review. Implementation is solid with proper Project/User/Task blocks, DefaultBlockLoader for auto-loading, and ContextAssembler for context assembly. All 7 tests pass and clippy is clean. Three observations noted for potential improvements: skill naming mismatch, repository scoping in get_by_type, and single project block limitation.
